title: Feedback Frequency

content.question: What metric should you be checking daily but currently check monthly?
sop: List your key metrics and how often you actually check each one right now.
Rate the Frequency 1-10 of your checks, daily is 10, quarterly is closer to 1 (see calculation, page 5)
Pick your slowest-checked, highest-impact metric and move it to a weekly or daily check.
Rate your Adjustment 1-10, how meaningfully you change behavior based on what you see (see calculation, page 5)
If Adjustment stays low even after increasing Frequency, the problem is follow-through, not visibility, fix that next.
questions: What metric should you be checking daily but currently check monthly?
What's the longest gap right now between an action you take and finding out if it worked?
If you found out today's results tomorrow morning instead of at month's end, what would you change starting this week?

definitions.def: When a system's output is fed back as input, allowing self-correction or self-amplification.

content.equation_example: Rate Frequency 1-10 (how often you check the metric) and Adjustment 1-10 (how meaningfully you act on it). Frequency 9 times Adjustment 4 equals 36 out of 100. Higher means frequent checks are actually translating into real course-correction. Lower means you're either checking rarely, or checking often and doing nothing with it.
content.closing_statement: Checking more often only helps if you act on it.

caption:
Principle Nº 165: Feedback Frequency

Faster feedback = faster growth.

Ask yourself: What metric should you be checking daily but currently check monthly?
